title: Two Sum
statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

bruteForce.approach: Check all pairs using nested loops

optimal.approach: Use a hash map to store complement values

interviewTips: Always clarify if the array is sorted. Mention the trade-off between time and space complexity.

title: Merge Intervals
statement: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals.

examples.explanation: Since intervals [1,3] and [2,6] overlap, merge them into [1,6].

optimal.approach: Sort by start time and merge greedily

interviewTips: Sorting is key. Always ask about edge cases: empty array, single interval, all overlapping.

title: LRU Cache
statement: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.

examples.input: LRUCache(2), put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1), put(3,3), get(2)
examples.explanation: Cache evicts key 2 when capacity is reached.

optimal.approach: HashMap + Doubly Linked List for O(1) operations

interviewTips: Explain why Map maintains insertion order in JS. In other languages, discuss doubly linked list + hashmap approach.

title: Valid Parentheses
statement: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

bruteForce.approach: Use a stack to match brackets

optimal.approach: Stack-based matching (same as brute force - already optimal)

interviewTips: Classic stack problem. Mention edge cases: empty string, single character, nested brackets.

title: Number of Islands
statement: Given an m x n 2D binary grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.

bruteForce.approach: DFS from each unvisited land cell

optimal.approach: BFS with queue for iterative approach

interviewTips: Discuss BFS vs DFS trade-offs. BFS is better for large grids to avoid stack overflow.

title: Reverse Linked List
statement: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
interviewTips: Draw diagrams. Show both iterative and recursive solutions. Discuss space complexity trade-offs.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
statement: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values.

optimal.approach: BFS is already optimal for level order

interviewTips: Standard BFS template. Know variations: zigzag, right side view, bottom-up.
